verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Each failure mode has root cause + owning task; Reboot incident 2026-07-21 captured; Feeds T5, T6, T9; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates committed red at lifeos a82c166 (5/5 failing: catalog absent), green at HEAD (5/5)
gate_result.ownership_evidence: isolation_failure_modes.json enumerates FM-01..FM-08 — unattended-upgrades kernel swap, in-session desktop upgrades, tmpfs profile-runtime, home residuals, host docker/kvm, volatile journald, the unsandboxed agent shell, and missing boot re-attach — each with a root cause and a concrete owning spine task (ARCHBP-109/111/072/078/084/110/066/093).
gate_result.incident_evidence: The 2026-07-21 reboot incident is embedded with its six-step timeline (06:31 kernel 7.0.0-28 swap removing the running -22, 19:44 snapd/accountsservice/chrome/vscode upgrades, 19:55/20:02 crash dumps, 20:00 full-upgrade, 21:28 reboot) and the honest facts: filesystem 100% intact, tmpfs profile-runtime wiped, durable transcripts survived, the host itself healthy.
gate_result.feeds_evidence: The catalog feeds T5 (residual elimination: FM-03/FM-04/FM-07), T6 (service migration: FM-05/FM-07), and T9 (update governance: FM-01/FM-02/FM-06/FM-08).
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: tests/archbp-063-failure-modes.spec.ts validates the four known breakage classes, per-mode root cause and owning task, the incident timeline and facts, and the T5/T6/T9 feeds in the published catalog.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-063 is complete: what breaks isolation today is enumerated as FM-01..FM-08 in the versioned failure-mode catalog, each with a root cause and an owning spine task; the 2026-07-21 reboot incident is captured verbatim as the empirical ground truth (kernel swap under a live session, tmpfs wipe, filesystem intact); and the catalog feeds the T5, T6, and T9 lanes that retire the failure modes.
